note: golden_hash filled by golden-parity.test.mjs --update. Synthetic worked examples (hand-verified arithmetic) -- split point, expected losses, expected primary losses, weighting value, and ballast value are licensed NCCI/state table values supplied as inputs, never vendored. Vector 1: multi-claim debit mod. Vector 2: single small claim credit mod. Vector 3: zero-denominator edge (finite gate).

output_payload.regulatory_basis: NCCI Experience Rating Plan Manual (published national experience rating formula: Mod = (Ap + W×Ae + (1-W)×Ee + B) / (Ep + B))
output_payload.note: Split point, expected losses, expected primary losses, weighting value, and ballast value are licensed NCCI/state rating-bureau table values supplied by the caller from their own NCCI experience rating worksheet -- this kernel never vendors or reproduces those tables, only the published per-claim split and mod formula.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: NCCI Experience Rating Plan Manual (published national experience rating formula: Mod = (Ap + W×Ae + (1-W)×Ee + B) / (Ep + B))
output_payload.note: Split point, expected losses, expected primary losses, weighting value, and ballast value are licensed NCCI/state rating-bureau table values supplied by the caller from their own NCCI experience rating worksheet -- this kernel never vendors or reproduces those tables, only the published per-claim split and mod formula.

vectors.name: zero_denominator_edge
vectors.description: No expected primary losses and no ballast (denominator zero) -- finite gate must return mod 0 and flag EXPMOD_ZERO_DENOMINATOR instead of dividing by zero.

output_payload.regulatory_basis: NCCI Experience Rating Plan Manual (published national experience rating formula: Mod = (Ap + W×Ae + (1-W)×Ee + B) / (Ep + B))
output_payload.note: Split point, expected losses, expected primary losses, weighting value, and ballast value are licensed NCCI/state rating-bureau table values supplied by the caller from their own NCCI experience rating worksheet -- this kernel never vendors or reproduces those tables, only the published per-claim split and mod formula.
